openmeter/notification/webhook.purpose: Contract layer for the notification webhook delivery subsystem: defines the composed Handler interface (WebhookHandler + MessageHandler + EventTypeHandler), all shared Input/Output types with validation, the typed error taxonomy, and the event-type registry. Implementations live in svix/ (production) and noop/ (Svix-unconfigured fallback); the secret/ child generates/validates whsec_ signing secrets. Callers import only this package.

patterns.name: models.Validator on every Input type
patterns.description: Every *Input struct implements models.Validator: Validate() collects field errors into []error, joins with errors.Join, and wraps with NewValidationError. A compile-time assertion `var _ models.Validator = (*XInput)(nil)` sits adjacent to the type.
patterns.example:
var _ models.Validator = (*CreateWebhookInput)(nil)
func (i CreateWebhookInput) Validate() error { var errs []error; ...; return NewValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)) }

patterns.name: Typed error hierarchy with Is* helpers
patterns.description: Each error category (ValidationError, NotFoundError, RetryableError, MessageAlreadyExistsError, UnrecoverableError) is a struct wrapping an inner error, with a New* constructor and an Is* predicate using the generic isError[T] helper. Handler methods never return raw errors.
patterns.example:
func IsValidationError(err error) bool { return isError[ValidationError](err) }
func isError[T error](err error) bool { var t T; return errors.As(err, &t) }

patterns.name: Handler interface composition over flat interface
patterns.description: Handler embeds WebhookHandler + MessageHandler + EventTypeHandler. New capabilities go on the appropriate sub-interface; both svix/ and noop/ must satisfy all three.
patterns.example: type Handler interface { WebhookHandler; MessageHandler; EventTypeHandler }

patterns.name: EventType registered in NotificationEventTypes
patterns.description: New event types are package-level vars using EventType{Name, Description, GroupName} literals in events.go and appended to NotificationEventTypes. Missing the append means the type is never registered with Svix at startup.
patterns.example: var EventTypeInvoiceCreated = EventType{ Name: InvoiceCreatedType, Description: InvoiceCreatedDescription, GroupName: InvoiceEventGroupName }

patterns.name: MaxChannelsPerWebhook enforced via constant
patterns.description: The hard channel limit is MaxChannelsPerWebhook (10) with ErrMaxChannelsPerWebhookExceeded in errors.go. Channel-count checks reference these constants, never inline literals.
patterns.example: var ErrMaxChannelsPerWebhookExceeded = fmt.Errorf("maximum number of channels (%d) per webhook exceeded", MaxChannelsPerWebhook)

patterns.name: Signing-secret validation delegated to secret/
patterns.description: CreateWebhookInput/UpdateWebhookInput validate a provided secret via secret.ValidateSigningSecret; the secret/ sub-package owns whsec_-prefixed HMAC secret generation/validation using crypto/rand and standard base64.
patterns.example: if err := secret.ValidateSigningSecret(*i.Secret); err != nil { errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("invalid secret: %w", err)) }

key_file_guides.role: All Input/Output types, Webhook/Message domain structs, ExpandParams, EventType, the three sub-interfaces and the composed Handler interface, plus MaxChannelsPerWebhook/DefaultRegistrationTimeout consts. The only file callers import.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a method to Handler without updating both svix/ and noop/ breaks compilation. Every new Input needs Validate() + a var _ models.Validator assertion.

key_file_guides.role: Full error taxonomy (ValidationError, NotFoundError, RetryableError w/ RetryAfter, MessageAlreadyExistsError, UnrecoverableError, ErrNotImplemented) + the generic isError[T] helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add domain error types in svix/ or noop/ — they belong here. Svix HTTP errors map to these via internal.WrapSvixError in the svix/ sub-package, not here.

key_file_guides.role: Registry of supported notification event types as package-level vars; NotificationEventTypes slice passed to RegisterEventTypes at startup (entitlements balance/reset, invoice created/updated).
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new event type needs a const block (Name+Description), a var EventType{...}, AND an append to NotificationEventTypes — missing the append silently skips Svix registration.

anti_patterns: Adding Svix SDK imports or Svix API calls to this package — all Svix logic belongs in webhook/svix/
Returning raw errors from Input.Validate() — always wrap with NewValidationError so HTTP 400 mapping works via GenericErrorEncoder
Defining new error types in svix/ or noop/ — all error taxonomy lives in errors.go
Adding a new Input struct without a var _ models.Validator compile-time assertion
Hardcoding the channel limit (10) inline instead of referencing MaxChannelsPerWebhook / ErrMaxChannelsPerWebhookExceeded

decisions.decision: Handler is a composed interface (WebhookHandler + MessageHandler + EventTypeHandler) rather than a flat 10+ method interface
decisions.rationale: Lets callers depend only on the capability slice they need and lets noop/ and svix/ be verified against each sub-interface independently at compile time.

decisions.decision: Typed error structs with Is* predicates instead of sentinel errors
decisions.rationale: Enables the HTTP encoder chain to pattern-match on error category (ValidationError→400, NotFoundError→404, RetryableError→retry) without importing Svix types into the HTTP layer.

decisions.decision: events.go holds all EventType definitions as package-level vars, not inside sub-packages
decisions.rationale: Keeps the canonical event-type list visible to all consumers (notification service, test helpers) without importing the Svix implementation.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new notification event type (e.g. invoice.voided)
